summary: This is an exemplary test suite. Coverage is comprehensive: every documented requirement (OS dimensions, language-version axes, feature flags, include/exclude forwarding, max-parallel, fail-fast, max_size validation, file loading, and full integration) has dedicated tests. Rigor is excellent — edge cases are systematically addressed including empty lists being omitted, missing keys vs. empty keys, boundary conditions (exactly-at-limit vs. one-over-limit for both custom and default max_size), error message content assertions, and both True/False/default variants of fail-fast. Design is clean: tests are organised into TDD-ordered named classes matching the stated development rounds, each class has a clear single responsibility, fixtures use pytest's tmp_path correctly, tests are independent with no shared mutable state, and naming is self-documenting. The integration test in Round 9 meaningfully exercises all features together with specific value assertions rather than just presence checks. The only minor omission is the absence of tests for the CLI main() entry point (stdout delimiter format, exit codes, argument validation), but everything the generator function itself can do is covered thoroughly.
